problem_clarity.summary: The agent is specialized for academic performance support: it identifies weak topics from assessment history, explains risk drivers, generates study plans and exam preparation schedules, creates practice quizzes, and provides retrieval-augmented tutoring via an in-app chat.
what_it_does: Personalized risk analysis by topic (based on recent results/history).
Weekly study planning (focuses on weak/high-risk topics first).
Exam timetable lookup plus day-by-day prep plan generation.
Smart practice quiz generation with auto-marking and mastery/recommendation outputs.
RAG chat support that answers questions using stored knowledge base content and provides citations.
problem_clarity.who_it_is_for: Students (and, by extension, institutions/lecturers) who need actionable study guidance rather than generic answers.

priority_reasoning.primary_priority: Academic underperformance intervention and measurable support pathways.
why_it_is_priority_1: Academic gaps compound over time; early guidance has outsized impact on outcomes and retention.
The domain is naturally KPI-driven (grades, completion, risk level), enabling evaluation and iterative improvement.
The system is built around concrete actions (plans, quizzes, prep schedules), aligning with the urgent needs of exam cycles.
tradeoffs: Current persistence is optimized for local/demo testing; production durability can be migrated to PostgreSQL later.

ai_leverage_efficiency.approach: Uses LLMs where they add value (planning text, tutoring explanations, quiz generation, RAG answers) and keeps deterministic logic for scoring, routing, and structured outputs.
where_ai_is_used_effectively: Agent routing to choose the right sub-agent behavior for exam/quiz/calendar/weak-topic/recommendation queries.
LLM-based generation of quiz questions in strict JSON format (with safe fallbacks if parsing fails).
RAG answer generation via `backend/rag` pipeline for course-relevant tutoring.
controlled_generation: Structured JSON outputs for quiz generation.
Safety guardrails applied in the agent prompt (refusal for final-exam leak requests, safe handling for non-course questions).
Deterministic planning components and normalization of risk/percentages.

technical_execution.architecture_summary: Next.js frontend + FastAPI backend, with modular routers and a provider abstraction for OpenAI/Anthropic. Retrieval is handled by a ChromaDB-backed RAG layer.
key_implementation_points: Backend routers: phase-based analytics/planning and phase-based quiz/exam endpoints, plus an agents router for chat routing.
Quiz generation and submission via `backend/routers/phase3.py` with strict-JSON parsing and fallback generation.
Authenticated endpoints via Clerk JWT verification (`backend/services/auth.py`).
Benchmark tooling: `benchmarks/live_benchmark.py` runs real executions; `benchmarks/evaluate_agent.py` converts rubric scores to 1..10,000 scale.

benchmark_method_reference.latency_scoring: Latency is measured per test and mapped into the same 1.0–5.0 rubric scale via a fixed latency-to-score mapping.

technical_execution.real_vs_demo_note: The benchmark kit now supports a non-dummy path that generates `benchmarks/results_real.json` from live agent executions; baseline Cursor Claude comparison requires supplying real baseline responses in the provided template.

readme_coverage: Local setup instructions for backend and frontend.
API surface description for the key endpoints.
A dedicated quest requirement compliance section mapping each requirement (1..7).
supporting_docs: Architecture docs under `docs/` (system architecture, API architecture, multi-agent workflow).
Benchmark docs and scripts under `benchmarks/` (including live benchmark runner and evaluation formula).
documentation_clarity.clarity_goal: Make it easy for reviewers to run, understand specialization, and verify evaluation methodology without reading the full codebase.

secret_handling: Secrets are stored in environment files (`.env` and `frontend/.env.local`) and are referenced via environment variables at runtime.
Repository publishing is protected by `.gitignore` entries that exclude `.env` and frontend env files and ignore build artifacts.
security_awareness.no_secrets_committed: Sensitive values should not be committed to the repo. `.env.example` contains placeholders only.
runtime_guardrails: Agent prompt includes safety constraints (no definitive financial/legal/medical advice, refusal for final-exam leak requests, safe handling of non-course questions, and privacy-aligned behavior).
verification_steps_for_submission: Run a final secret scan before publishing.
Ensure `.env` and `frontend/.env.local` are not included in the submitted package/public repo.
Verify commit history does not contain real keys.
